caption: The video showcases a close-up view of a tablet screen displaying an application called "Alpha Szenszor eNose v1.0.14." The app appears to be monitoring and analyzing data, likely related to sensor readings or environmental measurements. The screen displays a graph with multiple lines representing different channels, each showing fluctuations over time. The x-axis is labeled "Time (s)" and the y-axis is labeled "Current (μA)," indicating that the data being measured is current over time. The graph updates dynamically, with new data points appearing as the video progresses. The interface includes options for stopping the acquisition and selecting different channels, suggesting that the user can control the data collection process. The background is slightly blurred, focusing attention on the tablet's display. The video captures the real-time analysis and monitoring capabilities of the eNose application, highlighting its use in scientific or technical applications.
